news.headline: Fact-checking claims that Senegal’s football team were treated like ‘criminals’ at US airport
news.summary: Kicking off this Thursday, the 2026 FIFA World Cup has seen a fair share of criticism, before having begun. A new viral video has triggered more backlash, showing Senegal's national team being searched on the tarmac of a US airport with metal detectors, which internet users claimed shows the players

news.headline: At Least 12 Dead After Shooting by Multiple Attackers in Johannesburg
news.summary: The police said they were looking for more than 10 attackers, who had stormed a community of tin shacks. Investigators were still trying to determine the motive.

news.headline: Hero’s welcome for Omar Artan after Somali’s World Cup dreams dashed
news.summary: Referee Omar Artan was denied from the US, where he was set to become the first Somali to officiate at a FIFA World Cup.

news.headline: Pope Leo blesses new tower at Spain’s Sagrada Familia
news.summary:
Pope Leo on Wednesday blessed a giant new tower at Barcelona’s famed Sagrada Familia Basilica after celebrating mass inside what is now the world’s tallest church.
A choir of 600 singers performed at the service which lasted around 90 minutes and was attended by Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez

news.headline: Gwyneth Paltrow’s Israeli real estate ad goes viral
news.summary: Gwyneth Paltrow sparked widespread outrage by becoming the face of an Israeli real estate project.

news.headline: Wall Street counts down to SpaceX IPO
news.summary: Wall Street is counting down to what's expected to be the biggest market launch in history, as SpaceX prepares to go public. The IPO is expected to value the firm at $1.75 trillion, and make founder Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire. Also in the show - US inflation surges to its highest level
